subject: A bone-and-tusk pendant on a leather cord, marked with the red painted Gruul guild sigil, hung against a warm rust-colored backdrop
description: A primitive locket pendant made from a small animal skull or bone fragment flanked by curving tusks or fangs, strung on a worn leather thong. The face of the bone is painted with a crude red sigil — the Gruul Clans mark — daubed on like warpaint or blood. The pendant hangs against a warm, hazy rust-and-orange background suggesting firelight or smoke, with indistinct rocky textures behind. The whole composition feels rough, tribal, and handmade — a savage talisman rather than refined jewelry.
foreground: Bone-and-tusk pendant on a leather cord with a red Gruul sigil painted across it
background: Warm hazy rust-orange field, possibly firelight or smoke against rough stone
